name: Dairy Hollow House Skillet Sizzled Buttermilk
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Corn, canola, or peanut oil
recipeInstructions:
Dairy Hollow House Skillet Sizzled Buttermilk. Preheat oven to 375°F.
In a large bowl, combine cornmeal, flour, baking powder, salt, baking soda,
and sugar. If baking powder or soda appear lumpy, sift them in. Stir well
to combine. In a small bowl, whisk together buttermilk, egg, and oil.
Spray a 10-1/4 inch skillet with Pam. Put the skillet on medium heat,
add butter, and heat until the butter melts and is sizzling seriously.
Tilt the pan to coat the sides of the skillet. As the butter's melting,
quickly pour the wet ingredients into the dry, using a wooden spoon to
stir the wet and dry together with as few strokes as possible only as
many as needed to combine the two without beating or smoothing it out.
Scrape the batter into the hot, buttery skillet if it will sizzle as it
goes in. Place immediately in the oven and bake until golden brown on top,
about 25 minutes. Serve, hot, cut in wedges. Yield: 1 skillet or 8 large
wedges. Source: Chef du Jour, Crescent Dragonwagon. MM Waldine Van Geffen:
vghc42a@prodigy.com.
